meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Escape Tutorial Hell for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Break free from tutorial hell with this expert SaaS pricing checklist. Learn to ship faster for clients using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping value. By implementing a tiered SaaS pricing model—integrating Stripe via Antigravity and managing logic with Claude Code—you shift from passive learner to active builder. This checklist turns your technical anxiety into a repeatable, profitable client workflow that scales your development speed instantly.
content.intro:
You are drowning in a sea of 'To-Do list' apps, trapped in the infinite loop of tutorial hell. You’ve watched the videos, you’ve cloned the repos, but your bank account remains static. The gap between a tutorial and a production-grade SaaS isn't code—it's the courage to build a pricing engine that actually captures value.

Today, we kill the hesitation. We are moving beyond surface-level coding into the architecture of commerce. Using Antigravity to scaffold your infrastructure and Claude Code to handle the heavy lifting, you will transform your next client project from a prototype into a revenue-generating machine.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Tier Architecture
steps.description: Don't guess your prices. Architect a three-tier pricing structure (Starter, Pro, Enterprise) based on usage limits rather than features to maximize conversion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SaaS pricing configuration schema in TypeScript that supports tiered billing, usage-based quotas, and Stripe-compatible metadata fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing.config.ts file that defines three tiers with distinct usage limits for API tokens and user seats.'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Checkout Infrastructure
steps.description: Automate the handshake between your app and Stripe. This removes the manual overhead of managing subscriptions for your clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a serverless function that initializes a Stripe Checkout session, passing in the user ID and selected price tier ID.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a stripe-webhook handler to update user subscription status in the database based on session completion.'

steps.title: Implement Middleware Access Control
steps.description: Protect your premium features with middleware. If the user doesn't have the subscription, they don't see the data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust middleware wrapper that checks user subscription status against the database before allowing access to premium API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the auth middleware to all /api/pro/* routes and ensure it redirects unauthorized users to the pricing page.'

steps.title: Automate Usage-Based Metering
steps.description: Track actual consumption to trigger upgrades. This turns your SaaS into a self-selling machine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a schema and trigger function to increment usage counters for API calls and sync them with Stripe's metered billing API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Write a function that records usage events in Supabase and sends a sync event to Stripe whenever a limit threshold is reached.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate the Billing Loop
steps.description: Finalize the production environment and test the full cycle from signup to billing success.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script that configures necessary Stripe environment variables and sets up the production database schema for billing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run a full end-to-end test suite simulating a user subscription upgrade and verifying database state updates.'

pro_tips: Always price by the unit of value (e.g., tokens, storage) rather than features to avoid 'feature bloat' management.
Use Stripe's Test Mode to simulate failed payments and ensure your app handles subscription downgrades gracefully.
Never hardcode pricing IDs; store them in environment variables or a remote config file managed by Antigravity.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your middleware to prevent churn due to minor credit card expiration issues.
Ship the billing page first. If you can't collect money, the rest of the application is just a hobby.
content.next_steps: Once the billing flow is live, focus on building the 'core loop'—the one feature that provides the most value to your specific user base.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for free and start building for scale. This framework is your ticket to professional-grade SaaS delivery that clients respect and pay for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
